table.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 2px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : left; background-color: white; border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.title: Minimum and Maximum # of times the item can appear in the instance

div.style: display: inline-block; background-color: #d9e0e7; padding: 6px; margin: 4px; border: 1px solid #8da1b4; border-radius: 5px; line-height: 60%

ul.li: Include all codes defined in urn:iso:std:iso:3166

Include these codes as defined in http://worldhealthorganization.github.io/smart-icvp/CodeSystem/DiseaseTargeted
CodeDisplayDefinition
YellowFeverYellow FeverYellow Fever
PoliomyelitisPoliomyelitisPoliomyelitis

Include these codes as defined in http://id.who.int/icd/release/11/mms
CodeDisplay
XM0N50Poliomyelitis vaccines
XM0VX8Poliomyelitis oral, monovalent live attenuated
XM0KZ1Poliomyelitis oral, trivalent, live attenuated
XM79H3Poliomyelitis oral, bivalent, live attenuated
XM5V19Poliomyelitis, trivalent, inactivated, whole virus
XM0N24Yellow fever vaccines
XM3418Yellow fever, live attenuated

description.value: Concepts specifying a patient's sex for administrative purposes.

copyright.value: This material derives from the HL7 Terminology (THO). THO is copyright ©1989+ Health Level Seven International and is made available under the CC0 designation. For more licensing information see: https://terminology.hl7.org/license.html

ul.li: Include all codes defined in urn:iso:std:iso:3166

description.value:
This value set defines a base set of codes for countries.
Note: The codes for countries are taken from [ISO 3166](https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html).
Value set for yellow fever and polio only
This value set includes codes from ICVP

text.value: Name of disease of condition vaccinated or received prophylaxis against
Relevant authoring reponsible for issuing the certificate, or for overseeing the administration center
